page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. The server operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include a GPU.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 381.77 GB/sec and write speeds at 181.15 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 23361.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 46214.8, both placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Static web serving throughput is also highly efficient at 100.03 GB/sec. Single-core performance and LLM inference speeds generally align with average tier results, though text generation for medium-sized models remains strong.

The ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads, though it lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. The integrated 2850 GB NVMe SSD provides high-speed local storage, reducing the need for external storage attachments, but the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a potential constraint for memory-heavy applications.
This instance is optimized for multi-threaded compute tasks, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and high-throughput Redis deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 22 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and LLM inference tasks fall within the average range. The server lacks x86 instruction sets like AVX-512 and features a relatively low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. This configuration is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated Graviton processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.

96.0 GB of system memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations performance
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for software compilation, database hosting, and static web serving
